title: Cyclosorus appendiculatus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus appendiculatus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or mosquito ferns. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, a large and diverse group of ferns. The genus Cyclosorus is often considered a synonym of the genus Cyclosorus is part of the subfamily Thelypteridoideae. The order Polypodiales is a part of the class Polypodiopsida, which is a division of vascular plants. The class Polypodiopsida is further divided into the division Pteridophyta, which contains all vascular plants that produce spores.

intro: Cyclosorus appendiculatus, also known as the appendiculate ladder fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
